title: Astronauts Come Home with Moon Treasures
summary: Four astronauts are coming back to Earth after a trip around the Moon. They are in a spacecraft called Orion and will land in the ocean near San Diego. The astronauts took many pictures and have exciting stories to share about what they saw. They flew farther from Earth than any people before, breaking a record. They saw the far side of the Moon, which is the part we never see from Earth, and looked at its craters and plains. The crew talked to reporters from space and said they have lots of data and good stuff to bring home. They are happy to return and share their adventures with everyone.

What part of the Moon did they see?
What do the astronauts have to share?

background_read: The Moon is Earth's only natural satellite, and people have been exploring it for many years. Astronauts travel in spacecraft to study space and learn new things. Missions like Artemis help scientists understand more about the Moon and space. When astronauts return, they bring back information that can help with future space trips.
Article_Structure: WHO: Four astronauts on the Artemis II mission
WHAT: They are returning to Earth after flying around the Moon, bringing back pictures, stories, and data
WHERE: They will splash down in the ocean off the coast of San Diego
WHY: To share their discoveries and adventures from the Moon with the world

perspectives.description: The mission shows how humans explore new places in space to learn and inspire others.
